articles.title: China Starting to Fulfill ‘Promises,’ U.S. Trade Representative Says
articles.summary: As President Trump and China’s leader, Xi Jinping, meet, the United States said China has promised to expand its purchases of farm goods and airplanes.

articles.title: Trump Was Flattering, Xi Was Resolute. The Difference Spoke Volumes.
articles.summary: In contrast to his rhetoric about China at home, President Trump spoke in conciliatory terms with Xi Jinping, the Chinese leader.

articles.title: Trump’s and Xi’s Body Language at the Summit Mirrored Their Styles
articles.summary: Although at odds over issues like trade and Taiwan, the U.S. and Chinese leaders met in Beijing with a show of friendly gestures.

articles.title: As Trump meets with Xi, security expert says China now faces the U.S. as a peer
articles.summary: Former national security official Rush Doshi says President Trump's sky-high tariffs on Chinese goods sparked a clash in which China prevailed. We look at the current state of U.S.-China relations.

articles.title: Trump tries to make deals in China. And, Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as Fed leader
articles.summary: President Trump is vying to make deals with China during his visit with President Xi Jinping. And, the Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as the next leader of the Federal Reserve.

articles.title: Cordial Trump-Xi meeting is a 'good sign,' says longtime U.S. diplomat
articles.summary: Longtime U.S. diplomat Richard Haass interprets President Trump's meeting with China leader, Xi Jinping. He said though the two have different priorities the cordial summit could be a 'good sign.'

articles.title: China's leader warns Trump that differences over Taiwan could lead to a clash
articles.summary: At the summit, the Chinese leader placed Taiwan, a key point of friction between the U.S. and China, at the center, calling it "the most important issue" between the two countries.

articles.title: House Again Blocks War Powers Vote to Halt Iran Conflict
articles.summary: But two vulnerable Republicans joined Democrats in the effort to force President Trump to win authorization from Congress, in the latest sign of G.O.P. jitters over the war.

articles.title: Supreme Court Allows Abortion Pill Access by Mail to Continue
articles.summary: A federal appeals court ruling against the Food and Drug Administration would have restricted access by mail to mifepristone.

articles.title: A Top U.S. Commander Dismisses Reports of Civilian Deaths in Iran
articles.summary: Adm. Brad Cooper said a strike on an Iranian school may have been the only civilian casualty incident of the war.

articles.title: Mail-Order Abortions Have Quickly Become Common
articles.summary: Pills shipped to states with bans are a reason abortion has increased since Roe v. Wade was overturned.

articles.title: Justices Allow Abortion Pill Access by Mail to Continue
articles.summary: Also, this is a big week for the art market. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

articles.title: British PM Keir Starmer's job is in jeopardy after his party was trounced in elections
articles.summary: After a victory for the far right in municipal elections, Britain's center-left ruling party is in turmoil over whether to replace the unpopular prime minister, Keir Starmer — and with whom.

articles.title: The 10 best songs competing at (a very contentious) Eurovision
articles.summary: Glitter, vocal gymnastics, on-stage flames — the show goes on on the Eurovision stage in Vienna, even though five countries are boycotting this year's contest due to Israel's participation.

articles.title: Native kids with disabilities were held in wooden boxes. Sweeping reforms are coming
articles.summary: State officials in New York say the Salmon River district's special education program confined young children with disabilities in wooden boxes. Parents weren't notified.

articles.title: Some plants have unusual genetics, which can help them weather cataclysmic events
articles.summary: Unlike humans, many plants have more than two sets of chromosomes. This trait may help them adapt to environmental upheaval, such as climate change.

articles.title: How AI is speeding new business creation, especially among Gen Z entrepreneurs
articles.summary: Entrepreneurs are turning to AI to speed the creation of new businesses, with Gen Z leading the way. That's according to a new report from the payroll software firm Gusto.

articles.title: The Supreme Court keeps abortion pill mifepristone available by telehealth
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled on Thursday that the abortion pill mifepristone can continue to be prescribed online or over the phone and sent through the mail.

articles.title: Linda McMahon defends dismantling the Education Department, shifting its work
articles.summary: The education secretary faced questions about the shrinking of her agency, limits on federal student loan borrowing and oversight of the education of students with disabilities.

articles.title: Cuba's power grid collapses and plunges eastern provinces into a major blackout
articles.summary: Cuba's aging power grid has eroded in recent years as it faces a prolonged economic crisis, made worse by a U.S. energy blockade of the island.

articles.title: Jury orders Boeing to pay $49.5 million to family of 737 MAX crash victim
articles.summary: A federal jury in Chicago awarded $49.5 million to the family of Samya Stumo, a young woman who was killed in the second of two Boeing 737 MAX crashes within months of each other in 2018 and 2019.

articles.title: A Philippine senator wanted by the International Criminal Court flees from Senate
articles.summary: A police investigation is underway in the Philippines, with suspicions that the incident was staged to help Sen. Ronald dela Rosa escape.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
